initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% each output cell must receive exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- row(R), col(C).

% background colour = colour of the topleft cell
1 { B : color(B) } 1 :- input(0,0,B).

% a cell is background iff it has colour B
isBg(R,C) :- input(R, C, B).

% forbid a nonbackground colour in a cell that is not background
:- output(R, C, V), not isBg(R, C), V != B.

% source cells (nonbackground) become background
output(R, C, B) :- input(R, C, V), not isBg(R, C).

% shift rightside pattern leftwards (column >= 7)
output(R2, C2, V) :- input(R, C, V),
                     not isBg(R, C),
                     C >= 7,
                     (C - 7) = C2,
                     R = R2,
                     row(R2), col(C2),
                     isBg(R2, C2),
                     R2 >= 0, R2 < N,
                     C2 >= 0, C2 < M.

% shift leftside pattern downright (column < 7)
output(R2, C2, V) :- input(R, C, V),
                     not isBg(R, C),
                     C < 7,
                     (R + 2) = R2,
                     (C + 7) = C2,
                     row(R2), col(C2),
                     isBg(R2, C2),
                     R2 >= 0, R2 < N,
                     C2 >= 0, C2 < M.

% a cell is a target of the pattern shift if it receives a nonbackground colour while being background originally
target(R, C) :- output(R, C, V), not isBg(R, C), V != B.

% copy background colour for cells that are not targets
output(R, C, B) :- input(R, C, B), not target(R, C).

% forbid more than one colour per cell
:- output(R, C, V1), output(R, C, V2), V1 != V2.

% hide auxiliary predicates
#show output/3.

tool_calls.name: edit_lines
tool_calls.result: edit_lines: Lines 11 replaced. Program now has 54 lines.

tool_calls.name: clingo_check
tool_calls.result:
clingo_check errors:
<block>:11:7-8: error: syntax error, unexpected :
